name: Ehukai Beach (Banzai Pipeline)
tagline: Pipeline is the most famous wave on Earth — watching from the sand puts you closer to the action than any surf comp broadcast.
parking: Tiny lot at Ehukai Beach Park or street parking on Ke Nui Rd; come early in winter
bestTime: Dec–Feb for massive Pipeline surf; summer is calm enough for swimming
talkStory: Ehukai means 'sea spray' in Hawaiian — fitting for the most famous wave on Earth. Pipeline was first surfed in 1961 by Phil Edwards over a reef just six feet deep; it has claimed multiple lives since. During the Pipe Masters each December, the sand literally vibrates from the impact of 15-foot barrels detonating yards from shore. In summer the same deadly reef disappears under flat turquoise water safe for swimming — a total Jekyll-and-Hyde beach.
